description: Trigger discrimination for the external CONTRIBUTING guide (this skill) vs new-hire onboarding (onboarding-doc-designer), the README (readme-craftsman), and the docs contribution toolchain (docs-as-code-architect).

cases.prompt: Write the CONTRIBUTING guide: setup, the fork/branch/PR workflow, how to run checks, the review process, and how to make a first contribution.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — a zero-to-merged guide for external contributors is a CONTRIBUTING guide. Not internal onboarding and not the README.
cases.reason: Designing the contributor's path from setup to merged change is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Design onboarding docs for a new engineer joining the team: first-week setup, architecture orientation, how we work, and a starter task.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — pin both ways) — an internal new hire joining the codebase is onboarding, not external contribution.
cases.reason: onboarding-doc-designer serves an internal joiner learning how-we-work. This skill serves an external contributor merging a change. Different audience and journey.

cases.prompt: Write the project README: what it is, who it's for, and a quickstart.
cases.reason: readme-craftsman writes the front door. It links to CONTRIBUTING, but the README serves all readers, not just contributors.

cases.prompt: Set up the docs edit workflow: edit-on-this-page, preview builds, and CI checks so contributors can change docs easily.
cases.reason: docs-as-code-architect designs the docs contribution mechanics/infra. This skill writes the human-facing guide content.
